data.narrative:
Dhruva Space is a Series B-stage Space & Aerospace company. The valuation framework draws from a comparable universe of 3 same-stage, same-sector private peers (Aalyria, Hubble Network, and Sift) and an anchoring set of 5 public Space & Aerospace companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) that trade at a median P/S multiple of 59.8x.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach. The 3 Series B Space & Aerospace comparables show valuations ranging from $274M (Sift) to $1.3B (Aalyria), with a median of $400M. This heterogeneity—a 4.7x spread between min and max—reflects meaningful variance in stage progression and operational maturity within the peer set. The public-comp anchoring method cannot be applied, as Dhruva Space's revenue is not publicly disclosed, preventing meaningful P/S multiple application.

Given the single-method triangulation, the valuation range converges tightly around the peer median. Analysis supports a range of $340M to $460M, with a central estimate of $400M. Key uncertainties include (1) the representativeness of the 3-peer sample for early-stage space technology, (2) relative operational progress versus Aalyria and Hubble Network, and (3) the magnitude of revenue assumptions underlying the peer public comparables. Additional disclosure of customer traction or revenue would improve confidence in valuation anchoring.
